message: Node started: Perform a technical audit of the relationship between Quantum Darwinism (QD) and Holographic Quantum Error Correction (HQEC). 1) Map three discrete correspondences: (a) Redundancy of information in environmental fragments vs. subregion encoding in AdS/CFT; (b) The role of entanglement entropy in defining 'observable' information vs. the Ryu-Takayanagi surface; (c) The mechanism of decoherence/classicality emergence vs. the recovery of bulk operators from boundary data. 2) Validate these against formal proofs (e.g., the relationship between the Petz map and error correction, and Zurek's pointer states). 3) Identify the critical gap: QD focuses on the proliferation of information to many observers (classicality), whereas HQEC focuses on the protection of information against local erasures (fault-tolerance). Provide the final answer in the requested FINAL_ANSWER/JUSTIFICATION format.
